Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
Off Calendar Aug-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF A. LA ROCCA SEA FOOD INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order, And Turnover Order is granted. No opposition has been filed. The moving party is to submit a form of order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Judge: Monica R. Wiley, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Katy Bersamin, #11722.

Nathaniel L. Dunn, Esq. appeared for plaintiff A. La Rocca Sea Food, Inc., a corporation.
No appearance by defendant Stazione LLC, a California limited liability company, dba Brio Ristorante.
Default was entered against defendant on December 28, 2009.
Dismissals with prejudice as to defendants Cafe Tiramisu, Anto Restaurant Group and The Napoli Group, were filed on March 23, 2010.
Doe defendants were dismissed on June 23, 2010.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on June 28, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff A. La Rocca Sea Food, Inc., a corporation, and against the Defaulted Defendant Stazione LLC, a California limited liability company, dba Brio Ristorante as follows:

Damages/Principal - $27,968.34.
Prejudgment Interest - $3,140.60.
Attorney's Fees - $1,000.00.
Costs - $425.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $32,533.94.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue And/Or Evidence Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Nathaniel Dunn, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: A. La Rocca Sea Food, Inc.
Michael Purcell, Atty. for Defendant and opposing party: Cafe Tiramisu

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff A. La Rocca Sea Food Inc.'s Motion For Issue And/Or Evidence Sanctions, Matters Being Admitted, And/Or Terminating Sanctions, And Monetary Sanctions is granted as it seek monetary sanctions. Monetary sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $937.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
